summary: iOS 🟥 Incorrect name and inaccessible settings after deleting device
scenario: Deleting a paired device from Device Settings causes incorrect name/connection status for remaining device and prevents opening its settings
description_clean: After pairing at least two devices with the app, turning off one device, then selecting the disconnected device and deleting it via device settings, the user is returned to the device home screen for the remaining device. However, the name and connection status shown are incorrect (they reflect the deleted device instead of the remaining one), and the user is unable to open settings for the remaining device. Restarting the app fixes the issue. Expected behavior: deleting a device from the app should not cause any issues for devices that remain.
steps: 1. Open the Poly Studio Mobile app on your Android device.
2. Pair at least two Bluetooth audio devices (e.g., Device A and Device B) with the app.
3. Verify both devices appear on the Home screen.
4. Turn off one of the paired devices (e.g., Device B) using its power button.
5. On the Home screen, tap the disconnected device (Device B) to open its Device Settings.
6. In Device Settings, select the option to delete/remove the device.
8. Observe the Home screen, which should now show the remaining device (Device A).
expected: After deleting one device, the Home screen should correctly display the remaining device's name and connection status. The user should be able to tap on the remaining device to open its Device Settings and access all features without issues.
actual: After deleting the disconnected device, the Home screen shows the remaining device but with the name and connection status of the deleted device. Tapping on the remaining device does not open its Device Settings. Restarting the app resolves the issue and displays the remaining device correctly.
